enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
1.1 These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Priority Sector
Lending - Targets and Classification) Directions, 2020.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Priority Sector Lending - Targets and Classification) Directions, 2020

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 1.2 These Directions shall come into effect on the day they are placed on the official
enriched_requirements.object: the day they are placed on the official

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Applicability
The provisions of these Directions shall apply to every Commercial Bank [including
Regional Rural Bank (RRB), Small Finance Bank (SFB), Local Area Bank] and
Primary (Urban) Co-operative Bank (UCB) other than Salary Earners' Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank [including Regional Rural Bank (RRB), Small Finance Bank (SFB), Local Area Bank] and Primary (Urban) Co-operative Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
3.1 In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
enriched_requirements.object: to them below:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) 'Urban Co-operative Bank' or 'UCB' means a Primary Co-operative Bank
as defined under Section 5(ccv) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949
read with Section 56 of the Act.
enriched_requirements.object: as defined under Section 5(ccv) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 read with Section 56 of the Act

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) "On-lending" means loans sanctioned by banks to eligible intermediaries
for onward lending for creation of priority sector assets.
enriched_requirements.object: to eligible intermediaries for onward lending for creation of priority sector assets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The average
maturity of priority sector assets thus created by the eligible
intermediaries should be co-terminus with maturity of the bank loan.
enriched_requirements.object: of the bank loan

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, foreign banks with less than 20 branches
have an option to reckon the Credit Equivalent of Off-Balance Sheet
<image>
Exposures (CEOBE) extended to borrowers for eligible priority sector
activities for achievement of priority sector target, subject to the condition
that the CEOBE (both priority sector and non-priority sector excluding
interbank exposure) should be added to the Adjusted Net Bank Credit
(ANBC) in the denominator for computation of PSL targets.
enriched_requirements.object: Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) in the denominator for computation of PSL targets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
3.2 All other expressions, unless defined herein, shall have the same meaning as
has been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the
Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory modification or re-enactment
thereto or as used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.
enriched_requirements.object: as has been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory modi

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
3.3 Banks must ensure that loans extended under priority sector are for approved
purposes and the end use is continuously monitored.
enriched_requirements.object: under priority sector are for approved purposes and the end use is continuously monitored

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The banks should put in
place proper internal controls and systems in this regard.
enriched_requirements.object: internal controls and systems in this regard

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, lending to Medium Enterprises, Social Infrastructure and Renewable Energy shall be reckoned for priority sector achievement only up to 15 per cent of ANBC.
enriched_requirements.object: sector achievement only up to 15 per cent of ANBC

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
5.2 The targets for lending to SMFs and for Weaker Sections shall be revised
upwards from FY 2021-22 onwards as follows:
Financial Year Small and Marginal
Weaker Sections target ^
Farmers target *
2020-21 8% 10% 2021-22 9% 11% 2022-23 9.5% 11.5% 2023-24 10% 12%
 * Not applicable to UCBs ^ Weaker Sections target for RRBs will continue to be 15% of ANBC or CEOBE, whichever is higher.
enriched_requirements.object: FY 2021-22 onwards as follows: Financial Year Small and Marginal Weaker Sections target ^ Farmers target * 2020-21 8% 10% 2021

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
5.3 UCBs shall comply with the stipulated targets as under:
Categories Primary Urban Co-operative Banks Total Priority Sector
60 percent of ANBC or CEOBE, whichever is higher, from FY2024- 25 onwards, with interim targets for the previous financial years as under:
enriched_requirements.object: targets as under: Categories Primary Urban Co-operative Banks Total Priority Sector 60 percent of ANBC or CEOBE, whichever

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All efforts should be made by banks to increase the Farm Credit
(as per para 8.1) higher than the NCF target.
enriched_requirements.object: to increase the Farm Credit (as per para 8

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Banks should not deduct / net any amount like provisions, accrued interest, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: amount like provisions, accrued interest, etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
6.2 For the purpose of calculation of CEOBE, banks shall be guided by the Master
Circular on Exposure Norms issued by Department of Regulation, RBI vide
DBR.No.Dir.BC.12/13.03.00/ 2015-16 dated July 1, 2015 and as updated from
time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: Master Circular on Exposure Norms issued by Department of Regulation, RBI vide DBR

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs shall be guided by the relevant provisions of the Master
Circular dated July 1, 2015 on 'Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy - UCBs'
issued by Reserve Bank of India.
enriched_requirements.object: relevant provisions of the Master Circular dated July 1, 2015 on 'Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy - UCBs' issued by Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
6.3 SFBs shall be further guided by Para 6.5 (ii to vii) of the Operating Guidelines
for Small Finance Banks issued by Department of Regulation (RBI/2016-17/81
<image>
DBR.NBD.
enriched_requirements.object: Para 6

conditions:
if banks subtract prudential write
off at Corporate/Head Office level
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
6.4 While calculating Net Bank Credit as above, if banks subtract prudential write
off at Corporate/Head Office level, it must be ensured that the credit to priority
sector and all other sub-sectors so written off should also be subtracted
category wise from priority sector and sub-target achievement.
enriched_requirements.object: credit to priority sector and all other sub-sectors so written off should also be subtracted category wise from priority sector an

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Wherever,
investments or any other items which are treated as eligible for classification
under priority sector target/sub-target achievement, the same should also form

conditions:
where the credit flow
is comparatively lower (per capita PSL less than ₹9
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
With effect from FY 2024-25, a higher weight (125%) shall be assigned
to the incremental priority sector credit in the identified districts where the credit flow
is comparatively lower (per capita PSL less than ₹9,000), and a lower weight (90%)
will be assigned for incremental priority sector credit in the identified districts where
the credit flow is comparatively higher (per capita PSL greater than ₹42,000).
enriched_requirements.object: incremental priority sector credit in the identified districts where the credit flow is comparatively lower (per capita PSL less th

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The banks should continue to report the actual outstanding amount in QPSA returns
as hitherto.
enriched_requirements.object: actual outstanding amount in QPSA returns as hitherto

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)
The definition of MSMEs shall be as given in the Master Direction - Lending to
Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector FIDD.MSME &
NFS.12/06.02.31/2017-18 dated July 24, 2017 as updated from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: the Master Direction - Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector FIDD

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All
bank loans to MSMEs shall qualify for classification under priority sector lending.
enriched_requirements.object: priority sector lending

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
FSD.BC.32/24.01.007/2015-
16 dated July 30, 2015 on 'Provision of Factoring Services by Banks-
Review', inter-alia, the borrower's bank shall obtain from the borrower,
periodical certificates regarding factored receivables to avoid double
financing/ counting.
enriched_requirements.object: periodical certificates regarding factored receivables to avoid double financing/ counting

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the 'factors' must intimate the limits sanctioned
to the borrower and details of debts factored to the banks concerned, taking
responsibility to avoid double financing.
enriched_requirements.object: to the borrower and details of debts factored to the banks concerned, taking responsibility to avoid double financing

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Factoring transactions pertaining to MSMEs taking place through the Trade
Receivables Discounting System (TReDS) shall also be eligible for
classification under priority sector.
enriched_requirements.object: classification under priority sector

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of UCBs, the term "entities" shall not include institutions to which
UCBs are not permitted to lend under the RBI guidelines / the legal
framework governing their functioning.
enriched_requirements.object: UCBs are not permitted to lend under the RBI guidelines / the legal framework governing their functioning

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Since Housing loans which are backed by long term bonds are exempted
from ANBC, banks should not classify such loans under priority sector.
enriched_requirements.object: priority sector

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Investments made by UCBs in bonds issued by NHB / HUDCO on or after
April 1, 2007 shall not be eligible for classification under priority sector.
enriched_requirements.object: under priority sector

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Withdrawn w.e.f April 01, 2025
lakh per borrower to prepay their debt to non-institutional lenders (ix) Individual women beneficiaries up to ₹1 lakh per borrower (For UCBs,
existing loans to women will continue to be classified under weaker sections till their maturity/repayment.) (x) Persons with disabilities (xi) Minority communities as may be notified by Government of India from time
to time.
enriched_requirements.object: of India from time to time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
16.2 Overdraft availed by PMJDY account holders as per limits and conditions
prescribed by Department of Financial Services, Ministry of Finance from
time to time may be classified under Weaker Sections.
enriched_requirements.object: Sections

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The all-inclusive interest charged to the ultimate borrower by the originating
entity should not exceed the investing bank's MCLR + 10% or EBLR + 14%.
enriched_requirements.object: MCLR + 10% or EBLR + 14%

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) When the bank undertakes outright purchase of loan assets (eligible to be
classified under priority sector) from banks/ financial institutions, they must
report the outstanding amount actually disbursed to priority sector
enriched_requirements.object: actually disbursed to priority sector

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) IBPCs bought by banks on risk sharing basis relating to 'Export Credit' as
per Para 10, shall be classified from purchasing bank's perspective for
priority sector categorization.
enriched_requirements.object: bank's perspective for priority sector categorization

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, in such a scenario, the issuing bank
shall certify that the underlying asset is 'Export Credit', in addition to the due
diligence required to be undertaken by the issuing and the purchasing bank
as per guidelines in this regard.
enriched_requirements.object: asset is 'Export Credit', in addition to the due diligence required to be undertaken by the issuing and the purchasing ban

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
SFBs shall further be guided by the terms and conditions specified in Para 1.9
of DBR circular No.
enriched_requirements.object: the terms and conditions specified in Para 1

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
21.2 With effect from May 5, 2021, SFBs are allowed to extend fresh credit to
registered NBFC-MFIs and other MFIs (Societies, Trusts, etc.) which are members
of RBI recognised 'Self-Regulatory Organisation' of the sector, and which have a
'gross loan portfolio' (GLP) of up to ₹500 crore as on March 31 of the previous
year, for the purpose of on-lending to individuals.
enriched_requirements.object: allowed to extend fresh credit to registered NBFC-MFIs and other MFIs (Societies, Trusts, etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These limits shall be computed by averaging across four quarters
of the financial year, to determine adherence to the prescribed cap.
enriched_requirements.object: across four quarters of the financial year, to determine adherence to the prescribed cap

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks should maintain
necessary borrower-wise details of the underlying portfolio.
enriched_requirements.object: of the underlying portfolio

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks shall compute the eligible portfolio under on-
lending mechanism by averaging across four quarters, to determine adherence to
the prescribed cap.
enriched_requirements.object: under on- lending mechanism by averaging across four quarters, to determine adherence to the prescribed cap

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the
sake of business continuity and to ensure uninterrupted flow of credit to the
priority sector, banks may continue existing arrangement as per earlier guidelines
on co-origination, issued vide our circular No.
enriched_requirements.object: per earlier guidelines on co-origination, issued vide our circular No

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) In terms of press release: 2021-2022/177 dated May 7, 2021, an on-tap
liquidity window of ₹50,000 crore with tenors of up to three years at the repo
rate till March 31, 2022 has been opened to boost provision of immediate
liquidity for ramping up COVID-related healthcare infrastructure and services
in the country.
enriched_requirements.object: liquidity window of ₹50,000 crore with tenors of up to three years at the repo rate till March 31, 2022 has been opened to boost pr

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks may deliver these loans to
borrowers directly or through intermediary financial entities regulated by the
<image>
RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: borrowers directly or through intermediary financial entities regulated by the <image> RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The data on priority sector advances shall be
furnished by banks to FIDD, Central Office at quarterly and annual intervals as per
the reporting format (quarterly and annual), within fifteen days and one month,
respectively from the date of ending of each quarter and financial year in terms of
circular FIDD.CO.Plan.CO.BC.No.17/04.09.001/2016-17 dated October 6, 2016.
enriched_requirements.object: to FIDD, Central Office at quarterly and annual intervals as per the reporting format (quarterly and annual), within fifteen day

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of RRBs, the data on priority sector advances, in the above format, shall
be furnished to NABARD at quarterly and annual intervals.
enriched_requirements.object: at quarterly and annual intervals

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs shall be guided
by Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Filing of Supervisory Returns)
Directions - 2024 dated February 27, 2024, as updated from time to time, as
regards submission of data on priority sector advances.
enriched_requirements.object: Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions - 2024 dated February 27, 2024, as updated from time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Banks having any shortfall in lending to priority sector shall be allocated
amounts for contribution to the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF)
established with NABARD and other funds with NABARD/NHB/SIDBI/ MUDRA
Ltd., as decided by the Reserve Bank from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: contribution to the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) established with NABARD and other funds with NABARD/NHB/SIDB

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: shall be fixed by Reserve Bank of India from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India from time to time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Common guidelines for priority sector loans
Banks should comply with the following common guidelines for all categories of
advances under the priority sector.
enriched_requirements.object: common guidelines for all categories of advances under the priority sector

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Service charges: No loan related and ad hoc service charges/inspection
charges should be levied on priority sector loans up to ₹25,000.
enriched_requirements.object: sector loans up to ₹25,000

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Receipt, Sanction/Rejection/Disbursement Register: A register/ electronic
record should be maintained by the bank wherein the date of receipt,
sanction/rejection/disbursement with reasons thereof, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: bank wherein the date of receipt, sanction/rejection/disbursement with reasons thereof, etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The register/electronic record should be made available to all
inspecting agencies.
enriched_requirements.object: all inspecting agencies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Issue of acknowledgement of loan applications: Banks should provide
acknowledgement for loan applications received under priority sector loans.
enriched_requirements.object: applications received under priority sector loans

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Bank Boards should prescribe a time limit within which the bank
communicates its decision in writing to the applicants.
enriched_requirements.object: within which the bank communicates its decision in writing to the applicants

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: FIDD.CO.Plan.BC.No.5/04.0 9.001/2022-23 May 13, 2022 Lending by Commercial Banks to NBFCs and Small Finance Banks (SFBs) to NBFC-MFIs, for the purpose of on-lending to priority sectors 7.
enriched_requirements.object: Commercial Banks to NBFCs and Small Finance Banks (SFBs) to NBFC-MFIs, for the purpose of on-lending to priority sectors 7

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 09.01/2021-22 May 5, 2021 Priority Sector Lending (PSL) - On- lending by Small Finance Banks (SFBs) to NBFC-MFIs 10.
enriched_requirements.object: Lending (PSL) - On- lending by Small Finance Banks (SFBs) to NBFC-MFIs 10

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
FIDD.CO.Plan.BC.18 /04.09.01/2018-19
May 06, 2019 Priority Sector Lending - Targets and Classification 22.
enriched_requirements.object: Lending - Targets and Classification 22

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Withdrawn w.e.f April 01, 2025
May 10, 2018 Revised guidelines on lending to Priority Sector for Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks (UCBs) 27.
enriched_requirements.object: on lending to Priority Sector for Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks (UCBs) 27

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: UBD.BPD.(PCB).CIR.No.33/ 09.09.001/2011-12 May 18, 2012 Priority Sector Lending -Indirect Finance to Housing Sector.
enriched_requirements.object: Lending -Indirect Finance to Housing Sector
